---
title: "Claude Code for Researchers"
subtitle: "Materials from Ben Lira's one-hour demo at Wharton, 2026-04-16"
---

This site hosts everything from the Claude Code demo: the [slide deck](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1j67OxP7MQudUdp6t-OtCPzaASTS8nBktCXalzXrvC1g/edit), the [full runbook](demo_script.md) with every prompt used, a [CLAUDE.md template](TEMPLATE_CLAUDE.md), and [15 skills](skills/index.qmd) packaged for copy-paste install.

Everything is free to fork, adapt, and re-share.

---

## Start here

If you've never used Claude Code before:

1. **Install Claude Code.** [Follow the official install guide](https://docs.anthropic.com/claude/docs/claude-code).
2. **Install WisprFlow** (optional but recommended). Talking beats typing.
3. **Copy the CLAUDE.md template.** Grab [`TEMPLATE_CLAUDE.md`](TEMPLATE_CLAUDE.md), fill in your own role/projects/preferences, and save it to `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`. This is the single highest-leverage thing you can do.
4. **Install one skill.** Pick one from [the skills page](skills/index.qmd) that matches something you already do manually. `/pipeline-audit` is a good first choice if you write papers; `/data-analysis` is a good first choice if you don't.

## Installing a skill

```bash
# From this repo's root:
cp -r skills/pipeline-audit ~/.claude/skills/
```

Restart Claude Code. The skill is now callable as `/pipeline-audit`.

Some skills need placeholder variables filled in (paths, phone numbers, credentials) — grep for `{{` in the SKILL.md before first use. [The skills page](skills/index.qmd) lists what each one needs.

## Navigation

- [**Slides**](https://docs.google.com/presentation/d/1j67OxP7MQudUdp6t-OtCPzaASTS8nBktCXalzXrvC1g/edit) — the Google Slides deck Ben used during the demo
- [**Demo script**](demo_script.md) — every prompt Ben spoke into WisprFlow, with timing, fallback plans, and pre-scripted answers to audience questions
- [**Skills**](skills/index.qmd) — 15 slash commands you can install
- [**CLAUDE.md template**](TEMPLATE_CLAUDE.md) — the starter file for your own global memory

## About the demo

**Audience:** Wharton marketing department + invited colleagues. Third-year psychology PhD student as the target persona.

**Running example:** a joke-writing task with AI assistance, agency measures, and peer ratings — built live in Qualtrics, then rebuilt as a self-hosted web app to show why self-hosting matters for reproducibility.

**Format:** all live, no pre-recorded segments, one hour, Q&A in final 10 minutes.

## Reporting bugs

File issues on [the repo](https://github.com/lirabenjamin/claude-code-for-researchers). If a skill breaks or a placeholder is missing, flag it.

## License

Materials shared freely. Adapt, fork, improve, re-share. No warranty — you are responsible for checking what Claude produces. See the [demo script's Q&A section](demo_script.md#qa--41005100) for the specific answer on how to verify AI-generated analysis code.
